meta.title: Jonathan Brownstein — Data & Analytics Leader
meta.description: Independent consultant with 15 years of FP&A, analytics, and process-automation work inside global manufacturing and financial-services environments. Through Your Website In 7 Days, LLC I take on fractional, project-base

hero.description: Independent consultant with 15 years of FP&A, analytics, and process-automation work inside global manufacturing and financial-services environments. Through Your Website In 7 Days, LLC I take on fractional, project-based, and advisory engagements for clients that need a senior practitioner on forecasting, financial reporting, dashboard build-out, and AI or automation work without standing up a full internal team. Comfortable owning the technical build, and equally comfortable in the room with the finance lead or executive who needs the answer. All work is contracted and invoiced B2B through the LLC, with a standard MSA and W-9 on file.

items.title: Principal Consultant
items.context: Independent advisory and delivery practice serving mid-market and enterprise clients across financial services, insurance and reinsurance, and industrial manufacturing
items.intro: Engagements run from short fixed-scope projects through multi-month embedded analyst or fractional FP&A work. Discovery, written scope, milestone-driven delivery, documented handoff. Recent representative work:

subsections.title: Forecasting, Planning, and Process Automation
bullets: Designed and put into production an automated ARIMA-based forecasting platform for a global reinsurance client. Runs on a scheduled pipeline and surfaces anomalies and trend movement before period close, so the finance and actuarial leads can react before the books shut instead of after. Replaced what had been a manual, spreadsheet-heavy reconciliation cycle.

Built statistical outlier and anomaly-detection frameworks using probability density function methods across platforms processing more than a trillion records. The same approach now backs the client's variance-driver analysis and acts as an early warning on cost and revenue drift.

Designed a period-over-period valuation extract comparison architecture, a repeatable framework for movement analysis across time periods and source systems. Other teams inside the client have since extended it for their own variance and trend work.

subsections.title: Executive Reporting, Cost Analytics, and Special Projects
bullets: Build and maintain the Power BI, Tableau, and SSRS dashboards that finance, actuarial leadership, and the executive group use to read KPIs, variances, and financial movement. Translating model output and messy multi-source data into a clean one-page narrative is the part of the work clients lean on me for most.
Led a classification machine learning rebuild of a regulated reporting workflow. Materially improved accuracy, consistency, and audit readiness; the client's former CEO recognized the result.

Architected the data-lineage reference standard sitting under a client's strategic data warehouse, and set the engineering and analytics standards on metadata, lineage, data quality, and audit controls across the broader analytics function.
Run ad-hoc and special-project analyses on a recurring cadence, scoping the work with finance and business sponsors and walking senior leaders through the recommendation in plain language.

subsections.title: Team Leadership, Vendor Management, and AI Enablement
bullets: Lead small project teams of analysts and engineers across concurrent initiatives with architecture review, code review, and delivery checkpoints, and mentor on Databricks, Spark, SQL, and Python.

Evaluate and manage outside vendors and subcontractors on scope, contract terms, timelines, and quality. Comfortable being the working bridge between business stakeholders and technical teams when executive priorities are in play.
Scope AI and machine learning pilots against real production workloads, read vendor claims with a practitioner's eye, and give clients a clean in-or-out recommendation rather than a vague maybe.

items.title: Assistant Business Controller
items.organization: Chicago Pneumatic Company LLC (Atlas Copco Group)
items.context: FP&A, planning, and reporting partner inside a global multidivisional industrial manufacturer
bullets: Owned FP&A and business-partnering support for finance, sales, operations, marketing, HR, and regional leadership inside a global multidivisional industrial manufacturer, feeding the annual planning, monthly forecast, and executive reporting cycles.

Led a cross-functional reporting modernization that improved data accessibility, consistency, and operational efficiency across finance and operations, and stood up the data-governance and QA practices the team continued to build on.
Designed automated KPI dashboards and reporting frameworks for sales, operations, and finance leadership, replacing manual reconciliation work and shortening month-end close commentary cycles.
Evaluated and managed outside vendors and consultants on analytics, reporting, and finance-systems projects, with ownership of scope, contract terms, timelines, and delivery quality.

groups.title: Finance and FP&A: Annual and monthly planning, forecast process design, variance and cost-driver analysis, executive reporting, capital project and contract evaluation, P&L, balance sheet, and cash flow driver analysis, advanced Excel and VBA
items: Analytics and Modeling: classification machine learning, ARIMA and time-series forecasting, statistical outlier and anomaly detection, data mining, applied econometrics, Python ML stack (pandas, NumPy, scikit-learn, statsmodels)
Reporting and Visualization: Power BI, Tableau, SSRS, executive-level written and oral briefing
Languages and Platforms: Python, SQL, R, Databricks, Delta Lake, Spark, enterprise data warehousing, cloud data platforms, ETL and ELT pipelines
